steps.title: Track the structure
steps.content: The text opens with the Piast dynasty, then sets up a contrast: "some differentiate" between well-documented rulers (Otto Bolesławowic) and disputed figures (Siemowit). It then explains why — the chronicle's record of Siemowit relies on oral tradition, unlike records of later rulers.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
The whole passage exists to explain why historians treat Siemowit and Otto differently — i.e., why historians differ on one aspect of Polish history. That's (D).

(A) makes a comparison the text doesn't favor. (B) describes a process the text doesn't discuss. (C) misidentifies the focus.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
